title: Status Lines, Informational Responses, Content Rules, and Response Parsing · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent protocol team receives different fragmentation, peer, trust, timing, and failure constraints and transfers status lines, informational responses, content rules, and response parsing into a new response control-data and content decision parser. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of response control-data and content decision parser with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Parse HTTP version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and CRLF while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative.
Process zero or more permitted informational responses before the final response with bounded count and protocol-specific handling.
Parse HEAD response metadata as if GET were selected while never consuming response content bytes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Status Line Parser
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab protocol team handles case 2642 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound informational responses and transition HEAD, no-content, and CONNECT responses correctly; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse HTTP version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and CRLF while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Status Line Parser.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for parse http version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and crlf while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Status Line Parser, keep Status Line Parser connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only.
